A.brief_reason: The vertex formula for a parabola \(y = ax^2 + bx + c\) gives the maximum (or minimum) value at \(x = - rac{b}{2a}\). Here, \(a = -16\), \(b = 80\), so the time at max height is \(t = - rac{80}{2(-16)} = 2.5\). Plugging \(t = 2.5\) into the equation gives the max height as \(81\).
